Comparison

SpiniX vs Flex Rewards

Both use QR codes. Both work without a POS. The core difference: Flex Rewards digitizes the stamp card mechanic with multi-card programs. SpiniX replaces stamps with a gamified spin wheel that includes review generation and email automation.

SpiniX vs Flex Rewards: the short version

SpiniX vs Flex Rewards: feature comparison

Flex RewardsSpiniX
Price €29/mo $36/mo (all features)
Free tier 90% off first month Free until 50 customers
POS required No No
Card/program types Spin wheel only Stamps, prepaid, membership, passport
Apple/Google Wallet Yes (passes with push) Not mentioned
QR enrollment Yes (no app needed) Yes (no app needed)
Push notifications Via Wallet pass Unlimited
Email notifications Automated 6-email sequence Unlimited
Core loyalty mechanic Variable rewards (spin wheel) Deterministic stamps (1-100)
Gamification Core mechanic on every scan No
Google review generation 33% conversion rate No
Email marketing campaigns AI campaign builder Basic messages
WhatsApp integration Yes (SEA/UK markets) No
Multi-location Yes (Growth plan) Yes ($14.50/extra venue)
Welcome/birthday rewards Yes (email sequence) Yes (automated)
Survey rewards No Yes
Referral program No Yes
Analytics Dashboard + weekly digest Real-time reports

  • • Multi-location

Flex Loyalty Plus+ $36/mo
  • • All features included

  • • Unlimited members
  • • Push + email
  • • QR codes
  • • 24/7 support

SpiniX vs Flex Rewards: where each wins

Flex Rewards wins: multiple card types

Flex Rewards supports stamp cards (1-100 stamps), prepaid cards, membership cards, and digital passports — all on one platform. You can run a coffee stamp card alongside a VIP membership program. SpiniX offers one mechanic: the spin wheel. If you need different loyalty programs for different customer segments, Flex Rewards has more variety.

Flex Rewards wins: free until 50 customers

Flex Rewards is genuinely free until your 50th customer signs up. No credit card, no time limit. For a new business testing whether digital loyalty works, this is risk-free validation. SpiniX offers a promotional first month but charges from the start.

SpiniX wins: gamification drives engagement

Flex Rewards uses deterministic stamps: "collect X stamps, get Y reward." SpiniX uses variable ratio reinforcement: every scan produces a different, unknown reward. Variable rewards produce 3x more dopamine than predictable ones. 73% of stamp-based loyalty members go inactive before completing their card. SpiniX maintains 46% enrollment and 21% return within 14 days.

SpiniX wins: Google review generation

Flex Rewards doesn't include review management. SpiniX builds review prompting into the post-reward moment: guest spins, wins, gets asked for a review at peak positive emotion. 33% leave one. A standalone review tool costs $50-200/month. SpiniX includes it at €29/month total.

SpiniX wins: Apple/Google Wallet passes

Flex Rewards doesn't mention Apple or Google Wallet integration — rewards live in-app or browser. SpiniX generates native Wallet passes that stay on the customer's phone with push notification capability. Wallet passes have no "download an app" friction and persist even after browser tabs close.

SpiniX vs Flex Rewards: FAQ

Does Flex Rewards support Apple/Google Wallet?

Based on available information, Flex Rewards doesn't mention Apple or Google Wallet integration. Rewards are managed through their app or browser interface. SpiniX generates native Wallet passes that stay on the customer's phone permanently.

Can Flex Rewards generate Google reviews?

No. Flex Rewards focuses on stamp cards, memberships, and digital loyalty programs. For Google review generation, you'd need a separate tool. SpiniX includes review prompting with 33% conversion as a core feature.

Is the free-until-50-customers offer real?

Yes. Flex Rewards doesn't charge until your 50th customer signs up. Charities and non-profits may qualify for permanent free or discounted access. After the 50th customer, the $36/mo subscription starts.

Which is better for a cafe?

Depends on what matters more. For "buy 9, get 10th free" stamp card: Flex Rewards. For gamified engagement that produces Google reviews and builds an email list: SpiniX. Some cafes run both — stamp card for regulars, spin wheel for new guests.

Stamps or spins — which brings guests back?

Try SpiniX free for 14 days. Set up in 10 minutes. Compare engagement against stamp cards.
